demographics.living_situation: lives at home; highly distressed adult child present at the visit and made the call

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I can't see the floor, I can't hear what anyone is saying, and I am completely trapped in this dark room! (Adult child on the line: Mum has suddenly gone to pieces tonight — she's panicking, she nearly fell in the hallway, and she seems so confused, like she's lost her mind.)
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The adult child says their parent has become severely agitated and panicky tonight and nearly fell tripping over something in the hallway, and the family is frightened she has suddenly become confused.

symptom_narrative.onset: a gradual decline in eyesight and hearing over about the last two years, which the family says has become much worse recently; tonight it tipped over into a panic
symptom_narrative.duration: roughly two years of slow decline; the acute distress and near-miss fall are from this evening
symptom_narrative.character: the adult child describes trouble hearing high-pitched sounds and understanding speech when there is background noise, with the television turned up very loud; and painless blurring of vision with bad glare, difficulty reading fine print and trouble getting around the house at night
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the family says tonight is the worst it has been — the parent is extremely frightened, agitated and feels trapped
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: eyes and ears — vision and hearing
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: worse in dim light and in noisy rooms; gradually worsening over two years with a sudden crisis tonight
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing they have tried tonight; new glasses over the last while have given very little benefit
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: dim lighting, background noise and a loud television
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: a panic attack and severe agitation tonight; tripping over an unnoticed obstacle in the hallway (a near-miss fall); the family feels she seems confused
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: no sudden crisis like tonight before, but the vision and hearing have been slowly worsening for about two years with repeated changes of glasses
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: normally independent, but tonight paralysed by fear of falling; has been withdrawing socially and isolating because of frustration communicating

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: long-standing, slowly worsening eyesight and hearing over the past couple of years
past_medical_history.how_managed: repeated prescription-glasses changes with little benefit

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives at home; adult child is the main support and is very distressed; the parent has been withdrawing socially because of frustration hearing and seeing; the home is very dimly lit and the television is very loud

communication_style.typical_message_style: the adult child speaks for the parent, is frightened and frames the change as a sudden loss of the mind; the parent is agitated and hard to communicate with

access_barriers.other: profound combined hearing and vision difficulty makes direct communication with the patient very hard

objective_data_offered.value: on video the patient is squinting hard, leaning in and repeatedly asking people to repeat themselves loudly, visibly trembling and agitated
the adult child says their parent keeps the television extremely loud, cannot follow speech in a noisy room, and tonight tripped over something in the hallway she did not see
the adult child says the parent seems confused and frightened but is awake, talking and responds when they get close and speak up facing her
the home is very poorly lit and the television is very loud, described by the family
